User Story: As a Bible study seeker, I want to ask the AI study companion questions with optional scripture context, so that I get truth-seeking, multi-perspective answers that persist across the conversation.
Expected Behaviour: Parses JSON body for { message, context }. Rejects with 400 if message is missing or not a string (route.ts:56-58). If context is a non-empty trimmed string, appends a 'Current Scripture Context' block to the system prompt (route.ts:61-64). Loads the last 8 ChatMessage rows (desc by createdAt), reverses them to chronological order, and maps to {role, content} (route.ts:67-74). Persists the incoming user message to ChatMessage with context (route.ts:77-83). Calls ZAI.create() then zai.chat.completions.create with model 'glm-4.5', messages = [system+contextBlock, ...history, user message], thinking enabled (route.ts:85-96). On API error, inspects error message for '1113'/'Insufficient balance' and returns 503 with error+reply (route.ts:97-107); otherwise rethrows. Extracts reply from completion.choices[0].message.content with a fallback string (route.ts:109-111). Persists the assistant message (route.ts:114-120). Returns { reply, history: last 20 messages desc } (route.ts:122-128). Top-level catch logs and returns 500 with err.message (route.ts:129-135).
Edge Cases: message present but empty string -> falsy, returns 400 | message is a number/object -> typeof check returns 400 | context whitespace-only -> trim().length===0 so no context block added | no prior ChatMessage rows -> history empty, still proceeds | Z.ai returns no content -> fallback reply string used and SAVED as assistant message | Insufficient balance -> 503 but user message already persisted, assistant NOT persisted | history includes prior [RAG] user messages and role 'system' rows (cast loosely) injected into the conversation
Validation Rules: message: required, must be typeof string (route.ts:56) | context: optional, only used if trim().length>0 (route.ts:62) | NOTE: no length cap on message or context — unbounded input sent to LLM and DB | NOTE: no auth / rate limiting on the endpoint
Dependencies: DB model ChatMessage | z-ai-web-dev-sdk (ZAI.create, chat.completions.create) | @/lib/db (Prisma client) | model 'glm-4.5' | implicit Z.ai SDK credentials (SDK-managed, separate from ZAI_API_KEY used by zai-api.ts)
Assumptions: ChatMessage table is a single global conversation (no user/session scoping) — all users share one history | completion shape is choices[0].message.content | m.role values stored are valid LLM roles | thinking:{type:'enabled'} is accepted by glm-4.5 via the SDK
Notes: src/app/api/chat/route.ts:51 | src/app/api/chat/route.ts:56 | src/app/api/chat/route.ts:67 | src/app/api/chat/route.ts:77 | src/app/api/chat/route.ts:88 | src/app/api/chat/route.ts:99 | src/app/api/chat/route.ts:122

User Story: As a returning user, I want to fetch my recent chat history, so that the UI can render the prior conversation.
Expected Behaviour: Loads up to 50 ChatMessage rows ordered ascending by createdAt and returns { history } (route.ts:138-143). No input parameters, no error handling beyond Next default.
Edge Cases: Empty table -> { history: [] } | More than 50 messages -> only the oldest 50 (asc) returned, not the most recent | DB unreachable -> unhandled rejection -> Next 500
Validation Rules: No input to validate | NOTE: no auth; returns the global shared history
Assumptions: 50 oldest messages are the relevant slice for the UI | single global conversation

User Story: As a user, I want to clear the entire chat history, so that I can start a fresh conversation.
Expected Behaviour: Calls db.chatMessage.deleteMany() with no filter, deleting ALL ChatMessage rows, and returns { ok: true } (route.ts:146-149).
Edge Cases: Empty table -> still returns ok:true | Deletes everyone's messages (no scoping) | No confirmation / idempotent
Validation Rules: No input | NOTE: no auth, no scoping, no confirmation
Assumptions: Clearing the entire global table is the intended behaviour

User Story: As a researcher, I want to search the verse corpus by keyword and get the most textually similar verses ranked by score, so that I can find relevant passages without an LLM call.
Expected Behaviour: Reads q (trimmed) and topK (default 8) from query string (rag/route.ts:62-64). Returns 400 if q missing or length < 3 (rag/route.ts:66-68). Loads up to 500 Verse rows with book+chapter, ordered by verseNum asc (rag/route.ts:71-75). If no verses, returns { q, results: [] } (rag/route.ts:77-79). Builds docs with tokenized text (lowercase, split on non-alphanumeric, drop tokens <=2 chars and stopwords) (rag/route.ts:23-28, 82-88). Computes document frequency and IDF = log((N+1)/(c+1))+1 (rag/route.ts:90-101). Computes query TF (normalized by max term frequency) and cosine similarity per doc (rag/route.ts:104-111). Sorts desc by score, slices topK, filters score>0 (rag/route.ts:113-115). Returns { q, totalCorpus, results:[{ref,book,text,score}] } (rag/route.ts:117-126).
Edge Cases: q length exactly 3 -> allowed; length 2 -> 400 | topK non-numeric -> parseInt -> NaN -> slice(0,NaN) returns [] so results empty | topK negative -> slice(0,-n) returns leading items unexpectedly | query terms all stopwords/short -> queryTf empty -> all scores 0 -> empty results | corpus > 500 verses -> only first 500 by verseNum searched | verse text with no matching tokens -> score 0 filtered out
Validation Rules: q: required, min length 3 after trim (rag/route.ts:66) | topK: parseInt base 10, default '8' (rag/route.ts:64) | NOTE: topK not validated for NaN/negative/upper bound
Assumptions: 500 verses is sufficient corpus coverage | TF-IDF cosine over short verses yields meaningful ranking | v.book.slug and v.chapter.number always present (include guarantees relations)

User Story: As a study user, I want to ask a natural-language question and get an AI answer grounded only in retrieved corpus verses with citations, so that the answer is verifiable against scripture.
Expected Behaviour: Parses { question, topK=8 } (rag/route.ts:131-132). Returns 400 if question missing or not a string (rag/route.ts:134-136). Retrieves up to 500 Verse rows, runs the same TF-IDF/cosine pipeline as GET (rag/route.ts:139-165). If no scoring verse (top length 0), returns a canned 'could not find any verses' answer with empty sources (rag/route.ts:167-172). Builds a numbered context block from top verses (rag/route.ts:175-177). Calls glm-4.5 with a strict grounding system prompt + the question, thinking enabled (rag/route.ts:201-211). On API error containing '1113'/'Insufficient balance'/'429', returns 503 with error/answer and sources (rag/route.ts:212-220); else rethrows. Extracts answer with fallback (rag/route.ts:224). Persists a '[RAG] question' user ChatMessage and the assistant answer, both with context = comma-joined source refs (rag/route.ts:227-240). Returns { answer, sources:[{ref,book,text,score}] } (rag/route.ts:242-250).
Edge Cases: question empty string -> 400 | no verses match -> canned answer, no LLM call, no DB writes | topK non-numeric in body -> not parsed (used as-is); if string, slice coerces oddly | Z.ai 429 rate limit -> 503 path | LLM returns no content -> fallback answer saved as assistant message | POST has no top-level try/catch -> JSON parse error or DB write error -> unhandled 500
Validation Rules: question: required, typeof string (rag/route.ts:134) | topK: defaults to 8, NOT type-validated (rag/route.ts:132) | NOTE: no length cap on question; no auth
Dependencies: DB models Verse, Book, Chapter, ChatMessage | z-ai-web-dev-sdk | @/lib/db | model 'glm-4.5'
Assumptions: Retrieved top verses are sufficient grounding | LLM obeys the non-fabrication grounding rules | topK passed in body is a number

User Story: As a reader, I want an AI-generated structured summary of a chapter grounded in its verses, cross-references, and evidence, so that I quickly understand its context and contested points.
Expected Behaviour: Parses { bookSlug, chapterNum } (summarize/route.ts:10-11). Returns 400 if either falsy (summarize/route.ts:13-15). Looks up Book by slug -> 404 if not found (summarize/route.ts:17-18). Looks up first Chapter by bookId+number with ordered verses -> 404 if not found (summarize/route.ts:20-24). Joins verses into text (summarize/route.ts:26-28). Fetches up to 10 CrossReferences whose sourceRef OR targetRef startsWith '<bookSlug> <chapterNum>:' ordered by confidence desc (summarize/route.ts:31-36). Fetches up to 5 Evidence rows whose scriptureRef startsWith that prefix, with source, ordered by confidence desc (summarize/route.ts:39-44). Builds a structured system prompt embedding verses/cross-refs/evidence (summarize/route.ts:46-84). Calls glm-4.5 with thinking enabled (summarize/route.ts:88-96). On balance/429 error returns 503 (summarize/route.ts:97-104); else rethrows. Extracts summary with fallback (summarize/route.ts:107). Persists summary on the Chapter (summarize/route.ts:110-113). Returns { bookSlug, chapterNum, summary, crossRefs, evidence[mapped] } (summarize/route.ts:115-129).
Edge Cases: chapterNum = 0 -> falsy -> 400 (legitimately can't summarize chapter 0, but also blocks any 0 value) | chapterNum as string from JSON -> used directly in Prisma where number — type mismatch risk | book exists but chapter doesn't -> 404 | no cross-refs/evidence -> prompt says 'None yet in the database.' | LLM no content -> fallback summary still persisted on Chapter | balance error -> 503 but Chapter.summary NOT updated
Validation Rules: bookSlug: required, truthy (summarize/route.ts:13) | chapterNum: required, truthy — NOTE rejects 0 and does not coerce string->int (summarize/route.ts:13) | NOTE: no try/catch around req.json() or DB writes; no auth
Dependencies: DB models Book, Chapter, Verse, CrossReference, Evidence, Source | z-ai-web-dev-sdk | @/lib/db | model 'glm-4.5'
Assumptions: chapterNum arrives as a JSON number matching the Int column | scriptureRef prefix format '<slug> <num>:' matches stored refs | Overwriting Chapter.summary on every call is desired

User Story: As a reader, I want to retrieve a previously generated chapter summary without re-running the AI, so that I can display it instantly.
Expected Behaviour: Reads book and chapter from query string (summarize/route.ts:133-135). Returns 400 if either missing (summarize/route.ts:136-138). Looks up Book by slug -> 404 if absent (summarize/route.ts:139-140). Finds first Chapter by bookId + parseInt(chapter) -> 404 if absent (summarize/route.ts:141-144). Returns { summary, hasSummary: !!chapter.summary } (summarize/route.ts:145).
Edge Cases: chapter non-numeric -> parseInt NaN -> findFirst number:NaN -> no match -> 404 | summary null -> hasSummary false, summary null returned | book missing -> 404
Validation Rules: book: required (summarize/route.ts:136) | chapter: required, parsed via parseInt base 10 (summarize/route.ts:142) | NOTE: no NaN guard on parsed chapter
Assumptions: chapter query param is an integer string

User Story: As a contributor, I want to scrape the web for scholarly sources corroborating a query/scripture and auto-save them with credibility scores and evidence links, so that the knowledge base grows with vetted-ish corroboration.
Expected Behaviour: Wrapped in try/catch (scrape/route.ts:88-279). Parses { query, scriptureRef?, scriptureText?, num=8 } (scrape/route.ts:90-91). Returns 400 if query missing or not a string (scrape/route.ts:93-95). Dynamically imports webSearch/pageReader from zai-api (scrape/route.ts:98). Calls webSearch with query augmented by 'archaeology OR evidence OR ...' (scrape/route.ts:102-105). On search error: balance -> 503, else 502 (scrape/route.ts:106-118). If results empty/non-array -> 502 (scrape/route.ts:120-125). Takes top min(num,8) results; page-reads top 3 in parallel (scrape/route.ts:127-144). For each top result: derives domain, scores credibility, categorizes, extracts keywords, parses date (scrape/route.ts:147-160). Skips and marks if Source.url already exists (scrape/route.ts:163-167). If page HTML available, strips tags to text (slice 12000) and builds a 2-3 sentence summary (scrape/route.ts:174-194). Creates a Source row (scrape/route.ts:196-210). If scriptureRef provided, creates an Evidence (confidence = credibility*0.7, reviewState auto-corroborated, blindspot if credibility<0.6), a ReviewRecord, and an AuditLog (scrape/route.ts:214-252). Returns { query, scriptureRef, found, saved, sources[mapped] } (scrape/route.ts:257-272). Top-level catch -> 500 (scrape/route.ts:273-278).
Edge Cases: query present but empty -> falsy -> 400 | num large -> capped at 8 for processing (scrape/route.ts:129) | num <= 0 -> slice(0, min(num,8)) -> empty topResults -> saved 0 | URL parse failure -> domain 'unknown' (scrape/route.ts:149-155) | duplicate URL -> skipped:true pushed, no new Source/Evidence | scriptureRef absent -> only Source saved, no Evidence/Review/Audit | page-read fails -> snippet-only summary used | found count uses full searchResults.length but saved iterates only topResults
Validation Rules: query: required, typeof string (scrape/route.ts:93) | num: defaults to 8, capped via Math.min(num,8); NOTE not validated for negative/non-number | NOTE: scriptureRef/scriptureText accepted unvalidated and stored directly | NOTE: no auth — anyone can write Source/Evidence/AuditLog rows
Dependencies: DB models Source, Evidence, ReviewRecord, AuditLog | @/lib/zai-api (webSearch, pageReader) | env ZAI_API_KEY/ZAI_BASE_URL/ZAI_MODEL (used inside zai-api) | @/lib/db
Assumptions: webSearch results have url/snippet/name/date fields | credibility*0.7 is a meaningful evidence confidence | auto-corroborated state is acceptable to inject unreviewed data | Source.url uniqueness enforced (schema @unique)
Notes: src/app/api/scrape/route.ts:88 | src/app/api/scrape/route.ts:93 | src/app/api/scrape/route.ts:102 | src/app/api/scrape/route.ts:163 | src/app/api/scrape/route.ts:196 | src/app/api/scrape/route.ts:214

User Story: As a reviewer, I want to list the most recently retrieved sources with their evidence links, so that I can audit what the scraper has ingested.
Expected Behaviour: Loads up to 100 Source rows ordered by retrievedAt desc, including their evidences, and returns { sources } (scrape/route.ts:282-289).
Edge Cases: Empty table -> { sources: [] } | More than 100 sources -> only newest 100 | DB failure -> unhandled (no try/catch)
Validation Rules: No input | NOTE: no auth
Assumptions: 100-row cap is sufficient for review UI

User Story: As an operator, I want to batch-scrape corroboration across themes/verses/film-topics in one rate-limited run, so that the corpus grows automatically with sources and evidence links.
Expected Behaviour: Parses { mode='themes', limit=10, skipHealthCheck=false } (auto-grow/route.ts:27-30). Runs checkApiHealth unless skipped; if not ok, pushes a note but proceeds with free fallback (auto-grow/route.ts:43-49). Builds workItems by mode: themes (top `limit` Theme rows with one sample verse), film-topics (10 hardcoded queries sliced to limit), verses (intended to pick verses lacking evidence — see defect — then keyVerses containing 'Watchers') (auto-grow/route.ts:54-116). Dedupes by first 60 chars of query, then slices to limit (auto-grow/route.ts:118-127). For each item: webSearch(query,5); on error push to result.errors and continue; if empty skip++ (auto-grow/route.ts:130-146). Saves top 3 results: derive domain/credibility/category, skip existing Source, page-read only index 0, strip HTML, create Source (sourcesAdded++) (auto-grow/route.ts:148-207). If item.scriptureRef, create Evidence (confidence credibility*0.7, blindspot<0.6), then ReviewRecord via a re-query findFirst (evidenceAdded++) (auto-grow/route.ts:210-238). Sleeps 2000ms between items (auto-grow/route.ts:242). Per-item errors caught into result.errors (auto-grow/route.ts:243-245). Returns the GrowResult (auto-grow/route.ts:248).
Edge Cases: mode invalid -> none of the if-branches match -> empty workItems -> processed 0 | limit huge -> capped only by available data and slice(limit) | verses mode -> the NOT/in query maps evidence ids to '' (always-empty array) so the filter is a no-op; then a second query restricts to text contains 'Watchers' | no themes/verses in DB -> empty queue | API down -> proceeds with free fallback curated sources | ReviewRecord re-query findFirst could match a different evidence for the same source on reruns
Validation Rules: mode: optional, defaults 'themes'; NOT validated against allowed set (auto-grow/route.ts:28) | limit: optional, defaults 10; NOT validated for negative/upper bound (auto-grow/route.ts:29) | NOTE: no auth on a 5-minute, DB-mutating, externally-fetching endpoint
Dependencies: DB models Theme, VerseTheme, Verse, Book, Chapter, Evidence, Source, ReviewRecord | @/lib/zai-api (webSearch, pageReader, checkApiHealth) | env ZAI_* (in zai-api) | @/lib/db
Assumptions: Theme.verseLinks[0] gives a representative verse | Hardcoded film queries remain relevant | Skipping existing Source URLs is enough dedupe | Re-querying findFirst returns the just-created evidence
Notes: src/app/api/auto-grow/route.ts:26 | src/app/api/auto-grow/route.ts:43 | src/app/api/auto-grow/route.ts:87 | src/app/api/auto-grow/route.ts:130 | src/app/api/auto-grow/route.ts:211 | src/app/api/auto-grow/route.ts:229

User Story: As an operator, I want to preview corpus stats, API health, and available modes, so that I know what an auto-grow run would do before triggering it.
Expected Behaviour: Counts Theme, Verse, Source, Evidence rows; runs checkApiHealth; returns { stats, apiHealth, modes:['themes','verses','film-topics','all'], description } (auto-grow/route.ts:251-264).
Edge Cases: Empty DB -> all counts 0 | API down -> apiHealth.ok false with error | DB failure -> unhandled (no try/catch)
Validation Rules: No input | NOTE: no auth; checkApiHealth performs a real billable LLM ping (max_tokens:1)
Dependencies: DB models Theme, Verse, Source, Evidence | @/lib/zai-api checkApiHealth | env ZAI_*
Assumptions: A health ping per status request is acceptable

User Story: As a developer, I want a trivial health/hello endpoint, so that I can confirm the API route layer responds.
Expected Behaviour: Returns { message: 'Hello, world!' } as JSON (api/route.ts:3-5).
Edge Cases: No parameters; always returns the same payload
Validation Rules: No input
Assumptions: Placeholder/scaffold endpoint

Feature Name: Z.ai web search with free fallback (lib)
User Story: As an API consumer, I want a web-search function that uses Z.ai web-search-pro and gracefully falls back to curated sources when the account has no balance, so that scraping still returns something usable.
Expected Behaviour: POSTs to `${BASE_URL}/tools` with tool 'web-search-pro', request_id 'ws-<ts>' sliced to 30 chars, stream false (zai-api.ts:36-51). If res.ok, parses via parseToolResults (handles choices content array/text/string, data.data, data.results, raw array) and returns up to num if non-empty (zai-api.ts:52-56, 80-113). If error body contains '1113'/'Insufficient balance', falls through to free fallback; otherwise throws an Error with status+body (zai-api.ts:57-63). Catch: rethrows unless message includes 'Insufficient balance' (zai-api.ts:64-70). Free fallback matches query substrings against CURATED_SOURCES keys, else returns up to num general scholarly sources (zai-api.ts:298-341).
Edge Cases: API returns ok but unparseable shape -> results empty -> falls through to fallback | request_id truncated to 30 chars (may collide for rapid calls) | non-balance HTTP error -> thrown to caller | query contains multiple curated keys -> sources from all matching keys concatenated | no curated match -> 4 general sources (sliced to num)
Validation Rules: query passed straight to API/fallback (no validation) | num used in slice; not validated | NOTE: API_KEY has a hardcoded default literal (zai-api.ts:4)
Dependencies: env ZAI_API_KEY, ZAI_BASE_URL, ZAI_MODEL | global fetch | CURATED_SOURCES map
Assumptions: Z.ai /tools response matches one of the parsed shapes | 'Insufficient balance' / '1113' reliably appears in error bodies | Curated fallback URLs remain valid

User Story: As the web-search layer, I want to normalize the varied Z.ai /tools response shapes into SearchResult[], so that downstream code has a consistent structure.
Expected Behaviour: Inspects data.choices[0].message.content: if array, iterates parts — text parts parsed via parseSearchFromText (URL regex), web_search_result parts mapped to SearchResult (zai-api.ts:82-103); if string, parseSearchFromText (zai-api.ts:104-106). Else falls back to data.data / data.results / raw array if they are arrays (zai-api.ts:108-112). parseSearchFromText extracts unique http(s) URLs via regex, returns up to 8 with empty snippets and host_name derived from URL (zai-api.ts:115-127).
Edge Cases: content array with no recognized parts -> returns [] -> caller falls to fallback | text with no URLs -> [] results | data.data already in SearchResult-ish shape returned raw (not normalized) | malformed URL in web_search_result -> host_name '' via try/catch
Validation Rules: NOTE: data.data/data.results returned without field normalization | Regex-based URL extraction may capture trailing punctuation excluded by char class
Assumptions: Z.ai content is one of the handled shapes | URLs in text are valid search results

Feature Name: Z.ai page reader with direct-fetch fallback (lib)
User Story: As the scraping layer, I want to read a page's HTML via Z.ai extract-reader and fall back to direct fetch when the API is unavailable, so that I can extract content without API credits.
Expected Behaviour: POSTs to /tools with tool 'extract-reader', message content = url (zai-api.ts:135-149). If res.ok and choices content present, returns a PageReadResult wrapping the content as html (stringified if not a string) (zai-api.ts:150-165); if data.data.html present returns data (zai-api.ts:166). Any throw or non-ok falls through to directFetchPage (zai-api.ts:168-173). directFetchPage GETs the URL with a bot User-Agent and 15s AbortSignal timeout; if !res.ok returns null; else returns html + extracted title + publishedTime (zai-api.ts:180-207). Errors return null (zai-api.ts:204-206).
Edge Cases: API ok but no content and no data.html -> falls to direct fetch | direct fetch non-2xx -> null | fetch timeout (15s) -> AbortError -> null | title/publishedTime regex no match -> '' / undefined | non-string content stringified into html field (may not be real HTML)
Validation Rules: url passed directly to fetch — NOTE no allowlist/SSRF guard | NOTE: content slice/limits applied by callers, not here
Assumptions: extract-reader returns usable content | target URL is publicly fetchable | 15s is enough

User Story: As the auto-grow flow, I want to verify the Z.ai API is reachable and funded before a long run, so that I can warn/fall back appropriately.
Expected Behaviour: POSTs a 1-token 'ping' chat/completions request (zai-api.ts:224-236). If res.ok -> {ok:true, model} (zai-api.ts:237). Else parses JSON: error.code '1113' -> insufficient balance message; '1211' -> model not recognized; otherwise error.message or HTTP status (zai-api.ts:238-245). On thrown error -> {ok:false, error:e.message, model} (zai-api.ts:246-248).
Edge Cases: res not ok but body not JSON -> res.json() throws -> caught -> {ok:false, error} | error.code numeric vs string mismatch (compares to '1113' string) | network error -> caught with e.message | successful ping consumes a (small) billable request
Validation Rules: No input | Compares error.code to string literals '1113'/'1211'
Assumptions: error.code is a string | max_tokens:1 ping is representative of full availability

User Story: As a Bible study reader, I want to list all books in the canon with their chapters, so that I can navigate the scripture corpus.
Expected Behaviour: When no 'book' query param is supplied, queries db.book.findMany ordered by 'order' ascending, including each book's chapters ordered by 'number' ascending, and returns { books } as JSON.
Edge Cases: Empty corpus returns { books: [] } | Books with no chapters return an empty chapters array | A 'chapter' param with no 'book' param is ignored — listing branch is taken first
Validation Rules: No input validation; absence of 'book' param triggers the full-list branch
Dependencies: Prisma models: Book, Chapter | @/lib/db | DATABASE_URL env (sqlite datasource)
Assumptions: Book.order is populated for meaningful ordering | Returning all books and all chapters in one response is acceptable payload size

Feature Name: Fetch a chapter's verses with themes
User Story: As a reader, I want to open a specific book and chapter and read its verses with their theme tags, so that I can study a passage in depth.
Expected Behaviour: Looks up book by slug (findUnique, include chapters ordered by number asc). If book not found returns 404. Determines targetChapter from the 'chapter' param via parseInt(base 10) or falls back to book.chapters[0]?.number. If no targetChapter, returns { book, chapter: null, verses: [] }. Finds the chapter via findFirst by bookId+number; if not found returns { book, chapter: null, verses: [] }. Loads verses for that chapter ordered by verseNum asc, including themeLinks→theme. Returns book, chapter, and a mapped verses array with id, verseNum, text, geezText, ref (`${book.slug} ${chapter.number}:${verseNum}`), and themes (slug, name, weight).
Edge Cases: chapter param is non-numeric → parseInt returns NaN → findFirst with number: NaN finds no chapter → returns chapter: null, verses: [] | Book exists but has no chapters and no chapter param → book.chapters[0]?.number is undefined → targetChapter falsy → returns chapter: null | chapter=0 → parseInt yields 0 which is falsy → falls back to first chapter number (subtle: chapter '0' is treated as no chapter) | Verse with null geezText returned as null | Verse with no themeLinks returns themes: []
Validation Rules: Book must exist (slug unique) else 404 | chapter param parsed with parseInt base 10; no explicit numeric validation
Dependencies: Prisma models: Book, Chapter, Verse, VerseTheme, Theme | @/lib/db
Assumptions: Book.slug is unique (schema confirms @unique) | A valid chapter number is always > 0 (chapter 0 would be mishandled by falsy check)
Notes: src/app/api/scripture/route.ts:23 | src/app/api/scripture/route.ts:27 | src/app/api/scripture/route.ts:31 | src/app/api/scripture/route.ts:36 | src/app/api/scripture/route.ts:43 | src/app/api/scripture/route.ts:52

User Story: As a reader, I want to search verses, sources, and evidence by keyword, so that I can find relevant study material across the corpus.
Expected Behaviour: Trims q; if missing or length < 2 returns 400 { error: 'Query too short' }. Runs db.verse.findMany with text contains q (take 30, include book+chapter). Runs db.source.findMany with OR over title/summary/content contains q (take 20). Runs db.evidence.findMany with OR over claim/corroboration/notes contains q (take 20, include source). Returns q plus mapped verses (id, ref, book, chapterNum, verseNum, text), sources (id, url, title, domain, category, credibility, summary), and evidence (id, scriptureRef, claim, corroboration, alignment, confidence, nested source or null).
Edge Cases: q with leading/trailing whitespace is trimmed before length check | q exactly length 2 is allowed (boundary) | q of length 1 or empty returns 400 | SQLite 'contains' is case-sensitivity dependent on collation; default LIKE behavior applies | Special characters in q are passed literally to Prisma contains (no wildcards interpreted by Prisma's contains) | Evidence with null source field returns source: null
Validation Rules: q required and trimmed length >= 2, else 400
Dependencies: Prisma models: Verse, Book, Chapter, Source, Evidence | @/lib/db
Assumptions: Result caps (30/20/20) are acceptable; no pagination offered | LIKE substring matching is sufficient (no relevance ranking)
Notes: src/app/api/search/route.ts:9 | src/app/api/search/route.ts:10 | src/app/api/search/route.ts:15 | src/app/api/search/route.ts:21 | src/app/api/search/route.ts:32 | src/app/api/search/route.ts:44

User Story: As a reader, I want fast full-text search with highlighted snippets across verses, sources, and evidence, so that I can quickly locate and preview matching passages.
Expected Behaviour: Trims q; parses limit param (parseInt base 10, default 20). If q missing or length < 2 returns 400. Builds ftsQuery by splitting q on whitespace, wrapping each token in double quotes with inner quotes doubled (`""`), joined by spaces. Runs three raw queries via db.$queryRawUnsafe against FTS5 virtual tables verses_fts/sources_fts/evidence_fts, each JOINed to base table by rowid and MATCH ftsQuery, producing snippet() highlights. Verse query uses LIMIT = limit; source and evidence queries use LIMIT = Math.min(limit, 10). Each block is wrapped in try/catch that logs and leaves the array empty on error. Verse rows get rank = -index (negative array index, not bm25). Feature Name: Daily insight (rotated + cached, with on-the-fly generation)
User Story: As a daily reader, I want a single rotating insight for today with a passage, parallels, and a reflection, so that I have a consistent daily devotional.

Computes dayOfYear = floor((today - Dec-31-prev-year)/86400000) and picks verse = verses[dayOfYear % verses.length]. Builds ref. Feature Name: Auto-generate flashcards from a chapter via AI

User Story: As a learner, I want to grade a flashcard 0-5 and have its next due date scheduled by the SM-2 algorithm, so that I review material at optimal intervals.
Expected Behaviour: Parses { id, quality }. Validates id present and quality is a number in [0,5] else 400. Finds card by id (404 if missing). Applies SM-2: destructures easeFactor, interval, repetitions from the card. If quality < 3: repetitions=0, interval=1. Else: repetitions+=1; if repetitions===1 interval=1; else if ===2 interval=6; else interval=round(interval*easeFactor) using the PRE-update easeFactor. Then recomputes easeFactor = max(1.3, easeFactor + (0.1 - (5-quality)*(0.08 + (5-quality)*0.02))). Sets dueDate = now + interval days. Updates the card (easeFactor, interval, repetitions, dueDate, lastReviewed=now) and returns { card: updated }.
Edge Cases: quality exactly 0 or 5 are valid boundaries | quality 2.5 (non-integer) passes the number+range check and is used directly in EF math | quality < 3 resets repetitions and interval to 1 (lapse) but EF still adjusted downward | interval grows by round(interval*EF) only after repetitions >= 3 | EF floored at 1.3 | Quality outside 0-5 or non-number → 400 | Missing id → 400; unknown id → 404 | dueDate computed via setDate(getDate()+interval), month/year rollover handled by Date
Validation Rules: id required (truthy) | quality must be typeof number and 0 <= quality <= 5 | EF lower bound enforced at 1.3
Dependencies: Prisma model: Flashcard (SM-2 fields easeFactor, interval, repetitions, dueDate, lastReviewed) | @/lib/db
Assumptions: quality is intended to be an integer 0-5 though non-integers are not rejected | interval uses the easeFactor value from before this review (standard SM-2 ordering)

Feature Name: Review state transition (approve/reject/etc.) with checklist + audit
User Story: As a reviewer, I want to transition an item through the editorial state machine (submit-review, approve, reject, request-revision, archive), recording a checklist and an audit entry, so that only vetted content reaches the public site.
Expected Behaviour: Parses {itemType, itemId, action, reviewer='editor', reviewerRole='reviewer', notes, checklist}. Validates itemType against VALID_ITEM_TYPES (400 if invalid). Maps action→newState via stateMap; 400 if action unknown. If no checklist supplied and itemType=='evidence', auto-computes a checklist from the evidence (sourcesCited, credibilityTierMet [peer-reviewed/reputable-reference], claimTypeLabeled, perspectivesRepresented [>=1 link], noFabrication=true hardcoded, contestedFlagged=blindspot===true, authorityNotOverstated=true hardcoded). Finds latest ReviewRecord for {itemType,itemId} to compute newVersion=prev+1. Creates a new ReviewRecord with the new state/reviewer/checklist(JSON)/notes/version. Updates the underlying item's reviewState; sets publishedAt=now when newState=='approved' else null (for evidence/public-article/topic-page). Writes an AuditLog entry. Returns {record, checklist}.
Edge Cases: NO current-state guard: any action is allowed from any state — e.g. approve an item that was never submitted, or re-approve a rejected item, or approve directly from draft | reviewer/reviewerRole are caller-supplied free strings — a 'contributor' can pass reviewerRole='publisher' and approve their own item; no role enforcement | Approving then later reject/archive sets publishedAt back to null — a previously published-from item loses its publishedAt | itemType='source' passes VALID_ITEM_TYPES but has no item-update branch — ReviewRecord+AuditLog created but Source.reviewState never changes (and Source has no reviewState field in schema) | checklist auto-compute only runs for evidence; public-article/topic-page get null checklist unless caller supplies one | evidence schema reviewState enum (line 204) does NOT list 'needs-revision', yet request-revision writes that value to evidence.reviewState | approve sets evidence.publishedAt but does NOT create a PublicArticle (that's the separate publish endpoint) — 'approved' here means review-approved, not published | itemId not found for evidence checklist compute → evidence is null, checklist left undefined, but the subsequent db.update on a nonexistent id throws 500
Validation Rules: itemType ∈ {evidence, public-article, topic-page, source} else 400 | action ∈ {submit-review, approve, reject, request-revision, archive} else 400 | No validation that the transition is legal from the current state | No authentication; reviewerRole is trusted from the request body
Dependencies: ReviewRecord | AuditLog | Evidence | PublicArticle | TopicPage | review state machine | stateMap
Assumptions: Caller honestly reports reviewer/reviewerRole | noFabrication and authorityNotOverstated are confirmed out-of-band by a human (hardcoded true) | Approve is intended to be reachable from any prior state

User Story: As a publisher, I want to take an approved evidence record or topic page and create/update a corresponding public article, so that vetted content appears on the public site.

Builds slug=evidence-<last8 of id>, title, markdown body (scripture, claim, corroboration, source block with credibility), SEO fields, claimTypeSlug, perspectives, canonicalRefs. For itemType=='topic-page': loads topic page; 404 if missing; 400 if reviewState!=='approved'; copies slug/title/body/SEO/canonicalRefs. Any other itemType → 400. Upserts PublicArticle by slug: if exists, updates content + publishedAt=now + reviewState='approved'; else creates with hardcoded corroborationScore=0.5, confidenceScore=0.5, sourcesCount=1, perspectivesCount=perspectives.length. Syncs perspective links (deleteMany then recreate, weight 1.0) when perspectives present. Writes AuditLog 'publish' with actor='publisher'. Returns {published:true, article:{id,slug,title,publishedAt}}.

Feature Name: Public topic page detail with articles + perspective spread

Expected Behaviour: When slug present, loads TopicPage by slug including publicArticles filtered to reviewState==='approved' with perspectiveLinks→perspective. 404 if topic not found. Aggregates a Set of perspective slugs across all included articles. Returns topicPage with full body fields, an articles array (id/slug/title/subtitle/scores/counts/claimTypeSlug/perspectives slugs) and perspectiveSpread (unique perspective slugs).
Edge Cases: Topic page itself is returned regardless of its OWN reviewState (only its articles are gated to approved) — a draft/rejected/archived topic page is still served if you know its slug | Topic with no approved articles → articles:[], perspectiveSpread:[] | Unknown slug → 404 | Articles linked via topicPageId relation; an article published from evidence (no topicPageId) never appears here
Validation Rules: Article inclusion gated to reviewState==='approved' | Topic page itself NOT gated by reviewState | No auth (intended — public endpoint)
Dependencies: TopicPage | PublicArticle | PublicArticlePerspective | PerspectiveTag | review state machine (article gate only)
Assumptions: Knowing a slug is acceptable to reveal an unpublished topic page body
Notes: src/app/api/public/route.ts:56 | src/app/api/public/route.ts:57 | src/app/api/public/route.ts:60 | src/app/api/public/route.ts:66 | src/app/api/public/route.ts:69 | src/app/api/public/route.ts:74

User Story: As a public reader, I want a transparency page describing perspectives, claim types, credibility tiers, review states, and the review rubric, so that I can understand how content is vetted.
Expected Behaviour: Default branch (no list/slug/topic) returns {transparency:{title:'How We Vet', perspectiveTags (all, by name asc), claimTypes (all, by name asc), credibilityTiers (4 hardcoded), reviewStates (7 hardcoded), reviewRubric (7 hardcoded strings)}}.
Edge Cases: perspectiveTags and claimTypes come from DB and could be empty → returns empty arrays | credibilityTiers/reviewStates/reviewRubric are hardcoded constants, can drift from schema definitions | reviewStates list here omits nothing of the 7 states (matches), but is duplicated knowledge vs the review route's VALID_STATES
Validation Rules: No auth (intended — public)
Assumptions: Hardcoded tiers/states/rubric stay in sync with schema and review route

User Story: As a UI/consumer, I want all claim types ordered by name, so that I can label and display claim taxonomy.
Expected Behaviour: Returns all ClaimType rows ordered by name asc as {claimTypes}.
Edge Cases: Empty table → {claimTypes:[]} | No pagination (taxonomy is small)
Validation Rules: No auth (read-only taxonomy)
Assumptions: Taxonomy table is small enough to return unbounded

User Story: As a UI/consumer, I want all perspective tags ordered by name, so that I can display perspective badges and the perspective spread.
Expected Behaviour: Returns all PerspectiveTag rows ordered by name asc as {perspectives}.
Edge Cases: Empty table → {perspectives:[]} | No pagination
Validation Rules: No auth (read-only taxonomy)
Assumptions: Taxonomy table small

User Story: As an operator, I want a full JSON dump of books/themes/cross-references/sources/evidence/notes/chat history, so that I can back up or migrate the database.
Expected Behaviour: Runs 7 parallel queries: books (with chapters→verses ordered), themes (with verseLinks→verse→book/chapter), crossReferences, sources (with evidences), evidence (with source), notes, chatMessages (ordered createdAt asc). Maps themes to attach a flattened verses array with ref+weight. Returns a single JSON object with exportedAt timestamp and all collections.
Edge Cases: Entire DB loaded into memory and serialized at once — no streaming, no pagination; large datasets could exhaust memory/time | Exposes ALL private-layer data (sources, evidence, notes, chat history) with no auth — full data exfiltration vector | Includes Source.rawHtml/content (potentially large) bloating the payload | ReviewRecord/AuditLog/PublicArticle/TopicPage NOT included in the export (partial backup)
Validation Rules: No auth (defect — exposes entire private database)
Dependencies: Book | Chapter | Verse | Theme | VerseTheme | CrossReference | Source | Evidence | Note | ChatMessage
Assumptions: Caller is a trusted operator; private network assumed

User Story: As an operator, I want to check Z.ai API auth status, balance, and model availability, so that I can confirm the upstream LLM provider is reachable.
Expected Behaviour: Calls checkApiHealth() from @/lib/zai-api, spreads its result, adds timestamp (ISO now) and baseUrl (ZAI_BASE_URL env or default https://api.z.ai/api/paas/v4). maxDuration=15s.
Edge Cases: checkApiHealth throwing would surface as an unhandled 500 (no try/catch) | Response echoes the configured baseUrl — reveals provider endpoint | No auth on the health endpoint — health/balance info exposed
Validation Rules: No auth | maxDuration capped at 15s
Assumptions: checkApiHealth handles its own errors and returns a status object

User Story: As a reviewer, I want the system to auto-compute the editorial checklist for an evidence item, so that I can see at a glance whether it meets the publishing rubric.
Expected Behaviour: When no checklist is supplied and itemType=='evidence', loads the evidence (with source + perspectiveLinks→perspective) and computes: sourcesCited=!!source; credibilityTierMet = source.credibilityTier ∈ {peer-reviewed, reputable-reference}; claimTypeLabeled=!!claimTypeSlug; perspectivesRepresented = perspectiveLinks.length>=1; noFabrication=true (hardcoded, 'human reviewer confirms'); contestedFlagged = blindspot===true; authorityNotOverstated=true (hardcoded). Stored as JSON on the ReviewRecord and echoed in the response and audit details.
Edge Cases: noFabrication and authorityNotOverstated are ALWAYS true — they never actually gate anything | credibilityTierMet requires reputable-reference-or-higher; popular-journalistic/self-published count as NOT met but nothing blocks approval based on this — checklist is advisory only | contestedFlagged is literally just blindspot===true, not whether contested content was contextualized | If the evidence id doesn't exist, evidence is null and checklist stays undefined (no checklist computed, but the transition still proceeds and then the item-update throws) | Checklist is computed but NEVER enforced — an item failing every checklist item can still be approved
Validation Rules: Checklist is informational; it does NOT block the approve action | Only computed for itemType=='evidence'
Dependencies: Evidence | Source (credibilityTier) | EvidencePerspective | ClaimType (claimTypeSlug) | credibility tiers
Assumptions: A human separately confirms noFabrication and authorityNotOverstated | Credibility tiers peer-reviewed/reputable-reference are the 'authoritative' threshold
Notes: src/app/api/review/route.ts:102 | src/app/api/review/route.ts:109 | src/app/api/review/route.ts:111 | src/app/api/review/route.ts:113 | src/app/api/review/route.ts:114 | src/app/api/review/route.ts:116

User Story: As a study-engine user, I want to switch between the Study workspace, the editorial Review dashboard, and the Public site preview, so that I can move between authoring, gating, and previewing content.
Expected Behaviour: Three header buttons set `topTab` state ('study'|'review'|'public'). The active tab gets `bg-card text-foreground shadow-sm`; main content conditionally renders ScriptureReader/ChatPanel/right-panel, ReviewDashboard, or PublicSite. No API calls on tab switch itself (children fetch on mount). Icons: BookOpen/ShieldCheck/Globe.
Edge Cases: No loading/error state for the switch itself (pure client state) | Tab state is not persisted to URL or storage — refresh resets to 'study' (page.tsx:42)
Validation Rules: NOTE: no validation needed; closed set of 3 tabs via union type TopTab (page.tsx:37)
Dependencies: ScriptureReader | ChatPanel | SynergyView | ThemeExplorer | StudyTools | ReviewDashboard | PublicSite
Assumptions: Tab labels are hidden on mobile (`hidden sm:inline`), leaving icon-only buttons (page.tsx:223,234,245)

User Story: As a researcher, I want to full-text search verses, sources, and evidence, so that I can jump directly to relevant scripture or citations.
Expected Behaviour: Search icon toggles `searchOpen`. Input (autoFocus) runs `runSearch()` on Enter or 'Search' click → GET `/api/fts?q=<encoded>`; result sets a counts header (verses/sources/evidence) and lists up to 5 verses (clickable → handleVerseSelect + close) and up to 3 sources (external links). Empty/whitespace query returns early (page.tsx:93).
Edge Cases: Empty query: early-returns without fetch (page.tsx:93) | Searching state shows '...' on button (page.tsx:304) | NOTE: no error handling — fetch has no try/catch around res.json(); a non-JSON/500 response throws an unhandled rejection (only `finally` resets `searching`) | NOTE: no explicit empty-results message — only count line '0 verses · 0 sources · 0 evidence' | NOTE: evidence results counted in header but never rendered as a list
Validation Rules: Client-side: query trimmed and required before fetch (page.tsx:93)
Assumptions: searchResults typed as `any` (page.tsx:50) — no schema guarantees on shape

User Story: As a study user, I want a persistent indicator of which verse I have selected, so that I know what context the chat and synergy panels are using.
Expected Behaviour: When `selectedVerseRef` is set and topTab==='study', a bar shows the ref (mono) + first 80 chars of the verse text, with an X button to clear all three selection states. Selection toggles off if the same verse is re-clicked (handleVerseSelect, page.tsx:75).

Validation Rules: NOTE: none required
Assumptions: The trailing '...' is appended unconditionally even when text is <80 chars (page.tsx:276)

User Story: As an admin, I want to export the full database as JSON, so that I can back up the corroboration data locally.
Expected Behaviour: Clicking Download fetches GET `/api/export`, builds a Blob, and triggers a client-side download named `ethiopian-bible-backup-<YYYY-MM-DD>.json`. Hidden on mobile (`hidden sm:flex`).
Edge Cases: NOTE: no loading state, no disabled state during fetch, no error handling — exportBackup has no try/catch (page.tsx:104-114)
Validation Rules: NOTE: none
Assumptions: Export response is assumed always valid JSON

User Story: As an admin, I want to bulk-scrape corroboration for all themes and film topics, so that I can grow the evidence base in one action.
Expected Behaviour: Sprout button is disabled when `growing` or when `apiHealth.ok===false`. On click: native confirm() warning about time/credits, then POST `/api/auto-grow` with `{mode:'all',limit:8}`. On success: alert summarizing sourcesAdded/evidenceAdded/processed; on failure: alert(data.error). Errors caught → alert(e.message). Spinner shown while growing. Hidden on mobile.
Edge Cases: Re-entrancy guard: returns early if already growing (page.tsx:117) | Confirm cancellation aborts (page.tsx:118) | Disabled when API unhealthy (page.tsx:257) | Errors surfaced via native alert (not toast)
Validation Rules: confirm() gate before destructive/expensive op (page.tsx:118)
Assumptions: apiHealth may be null initially; `disabled={growing || (apiHealth && !apiHealth.ok)}` evaluates null as falsy so button is enabled until health resolves (page.tsx:257)

User Story: As an admin, I want the app to know whether the Z.ai API has credits, so that expensive operations are guarded.
Expected Behaviour: On mount, GET `/api/health` → `setApiHealth`. The result only gates the Grow button's disabled state and tooltip. Fetch errors are swallowed (`.catch(()=>{})`).
Edge Cases: Error silently ignored (page.tsx:70) | apiHealth stays null until resolved — no visible spinner/badge for health itself
Validation Rules: NOTE: none
Assumptions: model field exists in type but is never displayed (page.tsx:54)

User Story: As a study user, I want a resizable three-pane workspace on desktop and a single-panel switcher on mobile, so that I can read scripture, chat, and explore evidence comfortably on any device.
Expected Behaviour: An inline `isMobile` state (window.innerWidth < 768, on mount + resize listener) chooses layout. Desktop: ResizablePanelGroup with ScriptureReader (default 34, min 24), ChatPanel (34/24), right panel (32/24). Mobile: one of scripture/chat/right shown, switched via a 3-button bottom nav (Scripture/Chat/Explore).
Edge Cases: Resize listener cleaned up on unmount (page.tsx:65) | isMobile starts false → SSR/first paint renders desktop layout briefly before effect runs | Right panel content shared between layouts via rightPanelContent var (page.tsx:142)

Assumptions: Uses a local inline mobile check (page.tsx:58-66), NOT the shared useIsMobile hook in src/hooks/use-mobile.ts which is unused here

User Story: As a reader, I want to pick a book and chapter and read its verses, so that I can study the Ethiopian Bible text.

On book/chapter change GET `/api/scripture?book=&chapter=` loads chapter + verses (with cancellation guard). Prev/Next chapter buttons disabled at bounds. Book metadata bar (canon/category/date/author) shown. Notifies parent via onChapterChange. Loading shows 4 animated skeleton rows; empty shows 'No verses found.'

Hover reveals a Copy button → navigator.clipboard.writeText (stopPropagation so it doesn't select). First verse gets drop-cap styling (non-theme view).

User Story: As a learner, I want to ask the AI tutor questions about scripture and evidence with the selected verse as context, so that I get sourced, multi-perspective answers.
Expected Behaviour: On mount GET `/api/chat` loads history. Sending: optimistic user bubble, POST `/api/chat` with {message, context}. On !res.ok throws Error(e.error||'Chat failed') → sets `error` state shown in a destructive Card. On success replaces messages from server `history` (deduped, reversed) or appends `data.reply`. Auto-scrolls to bottom. Clear (Trash) → confirm() then DELETE `/api/chat`. Empty state shows suggested-question buttons. ⌘/Ctrl+Enter sends.

Feature Name: Public Site — topic article view & transparency page
User Story: As a search-audience reader, I want to read a corroborated topic page (or the 'How we vet' transparency page), so that I understand the content and its vetting.

Renders SEO preview card, state badges (approved/draft), perspective/refs/published badges, title, subtitle, film relevance card, markdown body, related articles with corroboration scores. When nothing selected but transparency loaded, renders the vetting page (credibility tiers, perspective tags, claim types, review rubric, lifecycle). Else spinner.

Assumptions: Theme persisted by next-themes (localStorage) by default

Feature Name: SEO metadata, robots, sitemap, brand mark
User Story: As a site operator, I want correct metadata, JSON-LD, robots rules, and a sitemap, so that the public resource ranks and is crawled correctly.
Expected Behaviour: layout exports rich Metadata (title template, OG, Twitter, icons, manifest, JSON-LD Organization+WebSite with SearchAction). robots.ts allows public routes and disallows /api,/review,/study,/admin for several bots. sitemap.ts (force-dynamic) emits static routes + DB-driven approved topics/articles/glossary/books, with try/catch fallback to static entries if DB not ready. Header renders an inline SVG brand mark with aria-label.
Edge Cases: sitemap DB failure → static-only entries (sitemap.ts:93-95) | robots disallows /study and /review (the actual app tabs) from indexing
Validation Rules: NOTE: server-generated, no user input
Dependencies: next Metadata API | db (Prisma) in sitemap | @/lib/db
Assumptions: Public routes like /topics,/articles,/glossary,/scripture exist as crawlable pages (referenced by robots/sitemap) though only '/' app shell is in scope here

User Story: As a developer, I want a toast system mounted globally, so that the app can surface non-blocking notifications.
Expected Behaviour: layout mounts <Toaster/> inside ThemeProvider. use-toast provides a reducer-based store with TOAST_LIMIT=1 and TOAST_REMOVE_DELAY=1000000ms; toast()/useToast()/dismiss exported. However, no in-scope feature component calls useToast/toast — feedback is done via window.alert/confirm and inline error states instead.
Edge Cases: TOAST_LIMIT=1 (use-toast.ts:11) — only one toast at a time | TOAST_REMOVE_DELAY=1000000ms ≈ 16.7 min before auto-removal after dismiss (use-toast.ts:12)
Validation Rules: NOTE: none
Assumptions: Toaster renders memoryState toasts
